desc: HS 2521 — limestone flux. The value-chain report names Karamoja as a domestic source, consistent with this trade being almost entirely regional rather than a major standalone import.
HS 2522 — quicklime, slaked lime and hydraulic lime (distinct from limestone, HS 2521)
HS 2804 — hydrogen, argon and other industrial gases (oxygen, argon — not separately split at 4-digit level)
HS 2819 — chromium oxides and hydroxides (closest fetchable code to chromic acid/chromium salts; not an exact match — sodium dichromate is a separate HS chapter not fetched)
HS 3206 — other colouring matter and pigments, incl. titanium dioxide-based
HS 3208 — paints and varnishes based on synthetic polymers (topcoat/primer/lacquer)

HS 7202 — ferro-alloys (deoxidation/chemistry additions). Not discussed with Uganda-specific cost or supplier detail in any of the three source documents — only a generic textbook description of alloying elements.
HS 7204 — ferrous waste and scrap (EAF charge input)
HS 7207 — semi-finished products of iron/non-alloy steel (slab, billet, bloom)
HS 7208 — flat-rolled, hot-rolled, ≥600mm (the wide segment the taxation policy review confirms has no local manufacturer)
HS 7209 — flat-rolled, cold-rolled, ≥600mm
HS 7210 — flat-rolled, clad/plated/coated, ≥600mm (shared by Galvanized Sheet, Galvalume, Pre-painted Coil and Tinplate)
HS 7211 — flat-rolled, hot- or cold-rolled, <600mm (narrow strip — matches Tembo Steels' actual production width)
HS 7213 — bars and rods in coils (wire rod)
HS 7214 — other bars and rods, iron/non-alloy steel (shared by Rebar and Merchant Bar)
HS 7216 — angles, shapes and sections of iron/non-alloy steel
HS 7301 — sheet piling and welded angles/shapes/sections of iron/steel (not sheet-piling-specific)
HS 7302 — railway/tramway track construction material
HS 7304 — seamless tubes, pipes and hollow profiles of iron/steel
HS 7601 — unwrought aluminium (Al-Zn alloy bath input). Same NPA/UDC report citation as zinc above — sourced from the UK.
HS 7901 — unwrought zinc (zinc ingots for galvanizing baths). The NPA/UDC value-chain report (para 599) names zinc, aluminium and hydrochloric acid as Phase V coating-line inputs sourced from the UK.
HS 8001 — unwrought tin (tin anodes for electrolytic tinning)
HS 8545 — carbon electrodes (EAF graphite electrodes). Not mentioned in any of the three source documents despite being a known major EAF cost item elsewhere.
HS 7305+7306 — welded tubes/pipes, large- and small-diameter combined
HS 1701 — cane or beet sugar and chemically pure sucrose.
HS 1703 — molasses from extraction or refining of sugar.
HS 1704 — sugar confectionery (not cocoa-containing).
HS 2202 — waters including mineral waters, sweetened or flavoured.
HS 2207 — undenatured ethyl alcohol (≥80% vol.) and denatured alcohol.
HS 2523 — Portland cement, aluminous cement and similar hydraulic cements (incl. clinker).
HS 2701 — coal and similar solid fuels (used as kiln fuel). Uganda imports from Tanzania.
HS 2709 — crude petroleum oils. Lake Albert future feedstock.
HS 2941 — antibiotics. Key API class; QCIL produces antibiotics locally using imported APIs.
HS 3002 — human blood, vaccines, toxins (biological products). All vaccines imported.
HS 3004 — medicaments (mixed or unmixed products for therapeutic use), dosed. Uganda imports + exports both.
HS 3102 — mineral or chemical fertilizers, nitrogenous (incl. urea). All imported.
HS 3104 — mineral or chemical fertilizers; potassic (MOP). All imported.
HS 3105 — mineral or chemical fertilizers N+P+K (incl. DAP, NPK). All imported.
HS 3901 — polymers of ethylene (HDPE; LDPE; LLDPE). 100% imported.
HS 3902 — polymers of propylene (PP resin). All imported; no domestic PP resin.
HS 3917 — tubes, pipes and hoses and fittings of plastics.
HS 3920 — other plates, sheets, film of plastics (non-cellular; not reinforced).
HS 3921 — other plates, sheets, film, foil of plastics (laminates).
HS 3923 — articles for conveyance or packing of goods (bottles, bags, boxes).
HS 3924 — tableware, kitchenware, other household articles of plastics.
HS 5201 — cotton; not carded or combed. Uganda major exporter of raw lint.
HS 5205 — cotton yarn (> 85% cotton). Fine Spinners and Nytil production.
HS 6109 — T-shirts, singlets and other vests, knitted.
HS 6211 — track suits, ski suits and swimwear (incl. workwear).
HS 6305 — sacks and bags of textile materials (incl. woven PP sacks).
HS 6810 — articles of cement, concrete or artificial stone (blocks, pavers, pipes).
HS 6907 — ceramic flags and paving; hearth or wall tiles (unglazed).
HS 7005 — float glass and surface-ground / polished glass.
HS 7010 — glass vials, ampoules, bottles for pharma. All pharma glass imported.
HS 7403 — refined copper and copper alloys (unwrought), incl. cathode
HS 7407 — copper bars, rods and profiles (incl. brass rod)
HS 7408 — copper wire
HS 7604 — aluminium bars; rods and profiles
HS 7614 — stranded wire, cable, plaited bands etc. of aluminium (ACSR)
HS 8507 — electric accumulators (EV and starter batteries)
HS 8544 — insulated electric conductors and cable
HS 8702 — motor vehicles for 10 or more persons (buses and coaches)
HS 8703 — motor cars and other vehicles for persons (incl. EVs)
HS 8708 — parts and accessories of motor vehicles
HS 8711 — motorcycles
HS 8716 — trailers and semi-trailers
HS 9018 — instruments and apparatus for medical/surgical use (incl. syringes).
